comment: Premise-level source/provenance audit. Each audit-premise from Assumptions.lean is judged individually; group-level approval is not sufficient. The K >= 2 condition is recorded as a human-verified implicit multiclass condition from nikhgarg on 2026-06-12.

assumption_theorem1_no_information_case.reason: Theorem 1(i) explicitly supposes that features provide no information, q(y,x)=Pr(y) for all x,y.
assumption_theorem1_no_information_case.source_location: Theorem 1(i), cached source text lines 761-768; appendix repeat lines 1454-1461

hnoInformation : ∀ x a, q x a = prior μ.reason: The paper's Theorem 1(i) states that x provides no information and q(y,x)=Pr(y) for all x,y.

assumption_theorem1_plurality_argmax_cla.reason: In the no-information regime the argmax rule assigns every point to a plurality/tie-broken argmax class.
assumption_theorem1_plurality_argmax_cla.source_location: Theorem 1(i), cached source text lines 764-768; argmax tie convention lines 229-230 and appendix lines 1544-1545

hplurality : ∀ a, prior μ a ≤ prior μ z.reason: The source says all data points are classified as a plurality class in the no-information case and fixes a consistent tie-breaking convention for argmax.

assumption_theorem1_perfect_classifier.reason: Theorem 1(ii) explicitly supposes that the classifier is perfect and identifies the true class.
assumption_theorem1_perfect_classifier.source_location: Theorem 1(ii), cached source text lines 786-788; appendix repeat lines 1466-1468

htruth : ∀ xy : X × Y, rule xy.1 = xy.2.reason: The source states the perfect-classifier case as q(z,x)=1 for the true label and 0 otherwise; the Lean premise is the induced decision-rule equality with the true label.

assumption_theorem1_argmax_rule_measurab.reason: The continuous formalization makes the standard measurability side condition explicit for the argmax rule used in Theorem 1.
assumption_theorem1_argmax_rule_measurab.source_location: Theorem 1 continuous argmax-rule statement, cached source text lines 759-763; formal measure-theoretic wrapper

hrule : Measurable argmaxRule.source_location: DSWG24DiscretizationBias.txt lines 759-763; formalization side condition for the continuous argmax rule
hrule : Measurable argmaxRule.reason: The paper states the continuous argmax rule under a data distribution; Lean exposes the standard measurability condition needed for the integrals in the continuous theorem.

assumption_theorem1_calibrated_classifie.reason: Theorem 1 is explicitly stated for calibrated classifiers.
assumption_theorem1_calibrated_classifie.source_location: Theorem 1 preamble, cached source text lines 759-763; appendix repeat lines 1451-1455

hcal : calibrated μ q.reason: The paper defines calibration as Pr(Y=y | q(y,x)=c)=c and states Theorem 1 for calibrated classifiers.

assumption_theorem2_at_least_two_classes.reason: Theorem 2 is in the paper's multiclass demographic prediction setting; nikhgarg confirmed on 2026-06-12 that K >= 2 is a human-verified implicit source condition.
assumption_theorem2_at_least_two_classes.source_location: Theorem 2 multiclass setup, cached source text lines 952-956 and 1908-1912; human validator nikhgarg, 2026-06-12

hK : 2 ≤ K.source_location: DSWG24DiscretizationBias.txt lines 952-956 and 1908-1912; human validator nikhgarg, 2026-06-12
hK : 2 ≤ K.reason: The paper states Theorem 2 for the multiclass setting. The human validator confirmed K >= 2 as an implicit source condition, not a proof-route certificate.

assumption_theorem2_more_rows_than_class.source_location: Theorem 2 statement, cached source text lines 952-956 and 1908-1912

hNK : K < N.reason: Theorem 2 states N > K, represented in Lean as K < N.

assumption_theorem2_positive_sample_coun.reason: Positive sample size is a direct consequence of the source theorem's N > K condition and the implicit nontrivial class count.
assumption_theorem2_positive_sample_coun.source_location: Theorem 2 statement, cached source text lines 952-956 and 1908-1912

hNpos : 0 < N.source_location: Derived from source condition N > K and implicit K >= 2; DSWG24DiscretizationBias.txt lines 952-956 and 1908-1912
hNpos : 0 < N.reason: The exposed positivity condition follows from the paper's N > K theorem condition together with the multiclass K >= 2 setting.

assumption_theorem2_reference_nonnegativ.reason: Theorem 2(iii) is stated for non-trivial reference distributions, which are probability distributions and therefore nonnegative.
assumption_theorem2_reference_nonnegativ.source_location: Non-trivial reference distribution definition and Theorem 2(iii), cached source text lines 926-927, 952-956, and 2058-2085

hpref_nonneg : ∀ y, 0 ≤ pref y.reason: The source defines the non-trivial reference object as a reference distribution; nonnegativity is the probability-distribution component of that source primitive.
